repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body: Subscripting from Python went through a rather generic index analysis that spent a lot of time doing things that are rarely needed, especially not for the simple indexing cases. Specialising the different cases makes it 25-40% faster.

comment_data.body: If I understand it correctly, this is pretty frequently called function (callgrind gives me around around 13M calls). Using something more efficient than list should not improve visibly performance?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -669,34 +675,91 @@ cdef memoryview_cwrapper(object o, int flags, bint dtype_is_object, const __Pyx_
 cdef inline bint memoryview_check(object o) noexcept:
     return isinstance(o, memoryview)
 
-cdef tuple _unellipsify(object index, int ndim):
+
+@cname('__pyx_memoryview_err_invalid_index')
+cdef int _err_invalid_index(item) except -1:
+    type_name = str(type(item))
+    PyErr_Format(PyExc_TypeError, "Cannot index with type '%.200U'", <PyObject*> type_name)
+    return -1
+
+
+cdef tuple _unellipsify_index_tuple(index_tuple: tuple, int ndim):
     """
-    Replace all ellipses with full slices and fill incomplete indices with
-    full slices.
+    Replace all ellipses with full slices and fill incomplete indices with full slices.
     """
-    cdef Py_ssize_t idx
-    tup = <tuple>index if isinstance(index, tuple) else (index,)
+    cdef Py_ssize_t first_ellipsis_index = -1
+    cdef Py_ssize_t idx, ellipsis_end, indices_from_ellipsis
 
-    result = [slice(None)] * ndim
     have_slices = False
-    seen_ellipsis = False
+
     idx = 0
-    for item in tup:
+    for item in index_tuple:
         if item is Ellipsis:
-            if not seen_ellipsis:
-                idx += ndim - len(tup)
-                seen_ellipsis = True
             have_slices = True
-        else:
-            if isinstance(item, slice):
-                have_slices = True
-            elif not PyIndex_Check(item):
-                raise TypeError, f"Cannot index with type '{type(item)}'"
-            result[idx] = item
+            if first_ellipsis_index == -1:
+                first_ellipsis_index = idx
+        elif isinstance(item, slice):
+            have_slices = True
+        elif not PyIndex_Check(item):
+            _err_invalid_index(item)
         idx += 1
 
-    nslices = ndim - idx
-    return have_slices or nslices, tuple(result)
+    if first_ellipsis_index >= 0:
+        result = [slice(None)] * ndim

comment_data.body: Of course changing the `result` to some kind of array will make #7530 obsolete but I will live with it ;-)

comment_data.body:
> something more efficient than list

It's a fixed size list storing object references, that'd be hard to beat.

comment_data.body: https://github.com/cython/cython/pull/7530 is a good idea, though. I hope you don't mind that I integrated it directly in https://github.com/cython/cython/pull/7529/commits/a8db84a6b91cbd8554f48aa27db68da8414e0a79 (rather than dealing with the merge conflicts).

commit.message: Separate out and simplify the common memoryview indexing cases in "_unellipsify()".
Reduce the chance of breaking an unrelated test on future changes to the memoryview code.
Benchmarks: Add a benchmark for memoryview slicing from Python.
Fix compilation with shared utility module.
Add a dedicated fast-path for integer indexing because it's the fastest thing a user can want.
Fix warning about undefined C evaluation order.

Avoid some for-loop overhead.
Borrowed from https://github.com/cython/cython/pull/7530

title: Separate out and simplify the common memoryview indexing cases in "_unellipsify()"
